The module docstring states explicitly: *"v1 limits: only `expectation` checks (ADR 0012) and only Snowflake connections have a `CheckRunner`... Both are 422s."* Any non-Snowflake connection (Unity Catalog, ADLS, S3) gets a `DryRunUnsupportedError` (422) from `POST /suites/{id}/checks/dryrun` (wired in `backend/app/api/v1/checks.py:313-343`). `docs/progress.md:134` and `:193` confirm this was never generalized ("runner dispatch generalises Week 5" — did not happen; frontend `DryRunPreview.tsx` comment even says "backend limits (Snowflake-only...)"). The column profiler, by contrast, genuinely is on all 4 (`profile_service.py:306-309` — `PROFILERS` dict covers `snowflake`, `unity_catalog`, `s3`, `adls_gen2`), so only the profiler row is correct.
- **Fix:** Change the Dry-run row to ✅ Snowflake only, `—` for UC/ADLS/S3, and add a footnote/issue reference (this should be filed as a tracked gap, similar to how #520 is already referenced for freshness/volume).

**2. `README.md` — "What it does" bullet implies dry-run works platform-wide**

- **File/line:** `README.md`, "What it does" → "Checks on 4 datasources" bullet: *"...plus a column profiler and dry-run preview."*
- **Claim:** Reads as a blanket capability alongside "4 datasources," implying dry-run works across all of them.
- **Reality:** Same as above — dry-run is Snowflake-only in code.
- **Fix:** Either scope the language ("column profiler on all datasources; dry-run preview on Snowflake") or drop the juxtaposition so it doesn't imply parity with the profiler.

### No other inaccuracies found

Everything else checked out precisely against code:

- `docs/notifications.md` — env var names (`TEAMS_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME`, `SLACK_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME`, `EMAIL_*`) match `backend/app/core/config.py` / `.env.app.example`; `alert_on` default `'warn'` matches `notification_service.DEFAULT_ALERT_ON` and `SuiteNotificationUpdate`; webhook write-only behavior matches `notifications.py`/`NotificationsPanel.tsx`; routing/dedup/suppression semantics match `routing.py`, `dedup.py`, `suppression.py` exactly, including the "operational failure always alerts" and "clean run resets baseline" nuances. The `#troubleshooting` anchor exists (`## Troubleshooting` heading) and `observability.md`'s link to it resolves.
- Note (not a doc bug, but a precision gap): the doc's allowed-hosts example `(webhook.office.com, hooks.slack.com, …)` conflates two separate allowlists — `teams_webhook_allowed_hosts` (default `webhook.office.com,logic.azure.com`) gates the **per-suite Teams override only**; Slack has its own separate `slack_webhook_allowed_hosts` (`hooks.slack.com`) and has no per-suite override at all. The doc's per-suite section correctly says "Teams webhook" only, so this is a minor wording imprecision in the Channels section, not a hard inaccuracy — worth a light polish but not blocking.
- `docs/scheduling.md` — cron 5-field validation, IANA timezone/DST semantics, no-backfill, 60s dispatcher tick, multiple schedules per suite (no unique constraint on `Schedule.suite_id`), and the stuck-run reaper all match `schedule_service.py`, `cron.py`, `celery_app.py`, and `db/models.py` exactly.
- `docs/feature-matrix.md` — check-kind × datasource gating (Custom SQL / Freshness / Volume = SQL-only) matches `expectationCatalog.ts` `SQL_ONLY_CATEGORIES`; issue #520 exists and matches the flat-file freshness/volume gap description; MCP tool count (8) and implied tool set match `backend/app/mcp/server.py`'s 8 `@mcp.tool` functions.
- `docs/datasources-checks.md` — field labels (`Warn ≥ / Fail ≥ / Critical ≥`, `Timestamp column`, `Minimum rows`/`Maximum rows`, `{batch}` placeholder) all match `expectationCatalog.ts` and `custom_sql.py` verbatim; the "fail or critical threshold required" for freshness matches `requireFailOrCritical`.
- `docs/observability.md` / `docs/orchestration.md` — the Settings → Webhooks admin panel, ADF token-reveal-toggle, and case-sensitive pipeline/DAG id matching all match `Settings.tsx` and `trigger_binding_service.py` (no case-folding applied).
- `mkdocs.yml` nav — every `docs/*.md` file is either in the nav or in `exclude_docs` (progress.md, post-v1-*-notes.md, compliance-posture.md); `--strict` build (`.github/workflows/docs.yml:34`) is protected by `validation: omitted_files: ignore` / `unrecognized_links: ignore`, so the nav restructure won't break CI.

### Verdict

**Conditional — 1 hard-adjacent finding (dry-run overclaim across 2 files).** The dry-run claim is a real overpromise in user-facing docs (feature-matrix.md table + README bullet) and should be corrected before merge — it tells users a capability exists on 3 datasources where the API will 422. Everything else in the branch is accurate and well-grounded in the code. Recommend fixing the two dry-run rows before merge; no other blocking issues.</result>

file.content:
# Runbook & FAQ

## Release checklist (v1)

- All CI gates green on `main`; no open release-blocking issues on the current milestone.
- Backend image built + pushed to GHCR with an **immutable** tag (not `latest`).
- DB migrations are backward-compatible; the migrate job runs `alembic upgrade head`
  **before** the app rolls.
- Deploy via the **Deploy** workflow (`workflow_dispatch`); verify `/healthz` 200,
  `/api/v1/me` 401 (auth enforced), SPA + deep links load.
- Docs site published (this site) and linked from the README.

Full deploy steps + verification: **[deploy/README.md](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/blob/main/deploy/README.md)**.

## Known limitations (v1)

- **GX-only** check engine (Databricks DQX deferred to v1.1); batch-oriented (not
  streaming).
- **Single tenant**, suite-level access sharing; workspace-admin is a config allowlist.
- Interactive **datasource browsing** (container browser, 3-level UC catalog picker) is
  deferred — you specify targets explicitly. JSON flat files deferred (CSV/Parquet in v1).
- Auth is **Azure AD only** (no API keys / service tokens yet — see ADR 0026 / #461).

## FAQ

**Is ADF/Airflow a datasource?** No — they're orchestration providers DataQ monitors and
can trigger from. You never write checks against them. See **[Concepts](concepts.md)**.

**Do I need Azure to run it locally?** No — local dev uses a dev-bypass auth and
docker-compose. Azure is one deployment target behind the app's seams (ADR 0010/0013).

**Where do failed-row samples go?** Stored with the result, **PII-redacted**, and purged
after a retention window — never written to logs.

**Can an AI assistant use DataQ?** Yes — 8 MCP tools at `/mcp` (Claude Desktop / Claude.ai
/ Copilot / Cursor), Azure-AD authenticated. See the README's MCP section.

Full deploy steps + verification: **[deploy/README.md](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/blob/main/deploy/README.md)**.

## Live smoke (deployed stack + harness data)

Automated, opt-in (never CI):

1. **API-level:** `DATAQ_API=https://<frontend-host> DATAQ_BEARER=<AAD token> python -m
   backend.scripts.e2e_smoke` — read + authoring round-trips against the live API
   (12 checks).
2. **Browser-level:** `E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL=https://<frontend-host> pnpm e2e` in
   `frontend/` — a headed one-time OIDC sign-in, then read-only specs (dashboard KPIs,
   live suite + checks, run-detail). See
   [frontend/e2e/README.md](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/blob/main/frontend/e2e/README.md).

Manual checklist (the mutating tail):

- Trigger a live suite run (Run now) on a harness suite → completes green on real
  warehouse/file data.
- Let a harness pipeline (ADF or Airflow) succeed → the bound suite auto-runs and
  correlates on **Results → Pipelines**.
- Force a failing run → the Teams/Slack/email alert arrives with the right severity;
  a repeat failure is deduped.
- MCP: point Claude Desktop at `https://<frontend-host>/mcp` and run the 4 canonical
  queries (what failed today / run suite X / why did pipeline Y fail / add a null check).

# Runbook & FAQ

## Release checklist (v1)

- All CI gates green on `main`; no open release-blocking issues on the current milestone.
- Backend image built + pushed to GHCR with an **immutable** tag (not `latest`).
- DB migrations are backward-compatible; the migrate job runs `alembic upgrade head`
  **before** the app rolls.
- Deploy via the **Deploy** workflow (`workflow_dispatch`); verify `/healthz` 200,
  `/api/v1/me` 401 (auth enforced), SPA + deep links load.
- Docs site published (this site) and linked from the README.

Full deploy steps + verification: **[deploy/README.md](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/blob/main/deploy/README.md)**.

## Known limitations (v1)

- **GX-only** check engine (Databricks DQX deferred to v1.1); batch-oriented (not
  streaming).
- **Single tenant**, suite-level access sharing; workspace-admin is a config allowlist.
- Interactive **datasource browsing** (container browser, 3-level UC catalog picker) is
  deferred — you specify targets explicitly. JSON flat files deferred (CSV/Parquet in v1).
- Auth is **Azure AD only** (no API keys / service tokens yet — see ADR 0026 / #461).

## FAQ

**Is ADF/Airflow a datasource?** No — they're orchestration providers DataQ monitors and
can trigger from. You never write checks against them. See **[Concepts](concepts.md)**.

**Do I need Azure to run it locally?** No — local dev uses a dev-bypass auth and
docker-compose. Azure is one deployment target behind the app's seams (ADR 0010/0013).

**Where do failed-row samples go?** Stored with the result, **PII-redacted**, and purged
after a retention window — never written to logs.

**Can an AI assistant use DataQ?** Yes — 8 MCP tools at `/mcp` (Claude Desktop / Claude.ai
/ Copilot / Cursor), Azure-AD authenticated. See the README's MCP section.

## In CI

The `frontend-e2e` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` spins up Postgres + Redis,
pip-installs the backend, runs migrations, seeds the demo data, launches
`uvicorn` on :8000, then lets Playwright start its own Vite dev server on :3000
(`VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET=http://localhost:8000`) and run the specs. No Azure /

input.new_string:
## In CI

The `frontend-e2e` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` spins up Postgres + Redis,
pip-installs the backend, runs migrations, seeds the demo data, launches
`uvicorn` on :8000, then lets Playwright start its own Vite dev server on :3000
(`VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET=http://localhost:8000`) and run the specs. No Azure /
real datasource credentials are involved — dev-bypass only.

## Live smoke (opt-in — never in CI)

Read-only specs in `e2e-live/` run against the **deployed** app with **real
OIDC**, activated only when `E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL` is set (CI never sets it):

```bash
E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL=https://<your-dataq-frontend-host> pnpm e2e
# optional: E2E_LIVE_SUITE="Retail Orders" (the suite the specs open)
```

First run opens a **headed** browser — complete the sign-in (MFA included);
the session's `sessionStorage` is captured to the gitignored
`e2e-live/.auth/session.json` and reused while fresh (~40 min; oidc-client-ts
keeps the user in sessionStorage, which Playwright's `storageState` cannot
capture — hence the custom setup). The specs then run headless and read-only:
dashboard KPIs, the live suite + its checks, a run detail.

Mutating live verification (trigger runs, alert delivery, MCP queries) is the
manual checklist in the docs-site **Runbook**. The API-level counterpart is
`backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py` with `DATAQ_API` + `DATAQ_BEARER`.

# Browser E2E (Playwright)

Drives the **real app in a browser** under dev-bypass auth against the seeded
demo dataset — the other half of the full-stack smoke (#128). The httpx API
smoke (`backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py`) proves `HTTP → service → DB`; this proves
the React UI a user actually clicks: `browser → Vite proxy → api → DB`.

> Live `test()`/runs against real Snowflake/S3/etc. stay out of scope (no creds
> locally — the documented deferred smoke). The connectivity tests here
> **fail-soft**; the specs assert the health/error path renders, not that a real
> datasource is reachable.

## Specs

| Spec                       | Covers                                                                                       |
| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `smoke.spec.ts`            | dev-bypass auth, app shell, sider nav                                                        |
| `connections.spec.ts`      | seeded connections grouped by type, "Test all" health path                                   |
| `suites.spec.ts`           | seeded suite → checks; check + suite authoring round-trips                                   |
| `results.spec.ts`          | seeded runs, run-detail drill-down, pipeline-runs feed                                       |
| `schedules.spec.ts`        | SchedulesPanel: add / pause / delete + invalid-cron 422 path                                 |
| `trigger-bindings.spec.ts` | TriggersPanel: bind pipeline / disable / remove (seeded ADF connection)                      |
| `notifications.spec.ts`    | NotificationsPanel: threshold routing persisted across reload; write-only webhook affordance |

antd Select gotchas (learned per spec, reuse these): rc-select pre-highlights
option 0 when nothing is selected, so `Enter` alone takes the first option and
`ArrowDown`+`Enter` takes the **second**; option nodes live in an off-viewport
rc-virtual-list measurement container, so `getByRole('option').click()` is
flaky — prefer keyboard navigation with the arrow-delta computed from the
currently rendered value (see `notifications.spec.ts`).

## Run it locally

1. Bring up the stack and seed the demo data:

   ```bash
   docker compose up -d            # postgres + redis + api + worker + frontend
   conda run -n dataq python -m backend.scripts.seed_dev
   ```

2. Install browsers once, then run:

   ```bash
   cd frontend
   pnpm install
   pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium
   pnpm e2e            # headless
   pnpm e2e:ui         # Playwright UI mode (watch/debug)
   ```

The config (`playwright.config.ts`) targets `E2E_BASE_URL` (default
`http://localhost:3000`) and **reuses** the already-running compose/`pnpm dev`
server on :3000. Point it elsewhere with `E2E_BASE_URL=… pnpm e2e`.

## In CI

The `frontend-e2e` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` spins up Postgres + Redis,
pip-installs the backend, runs migrations, seeds the demo data, launches
`uvicorn` on :8000, then lets Playwright start its own Vite dev server on :3000
(`VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET=http://localhost:8000`) and run the specs. No Azure /
real datasource credentials are involved — dev-bypass only.

(`VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET=http://localhost:8000`) and run the specs. No Azure /
real datasource credentials are involved — dev-bypass only.

gh pr create --title "test(e2e): opt-in live-smoke lane against the deployed app (never in CI)" --milestone "Week 7 — Deployment, hardening & docs" --body "## Summary
Discharges the **automatable half of the deferred live smoke** (the standing W1 caveat), per the W7 plan:

- **\`playwright.config.ts\`** — setting \`E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL\` flips the config to the read-only specs in \`e2e-live/\`: no \`webServer\`, \`workers: 1\`, project \`live-smoke\`. **CI never sets the variable → CI matrix untouched** (local lane re-verified: 17 passed).
- **\`e2e-live/global-setup.ts\`** — one **headed** real-OIDC sign-in per session (MFA-compatible); the captured **sessionStorage** is serialized to the gitignored \`e2e-live/.auth/\` and reused while fresh (~40 min). Custom because oidc-client-ts stores the signed-in user in sessionStorage (\`authClient.ts\` \`WebStorageStateStore\`), which Playwright's cookie/localStorage \`storageState\` cannot capture.
- **\`e2e-live/smoke.spec.ts\`** — 3 read-only specs: dashboard KPIs render live, the live suite (\`E2E_LIVE_SUITE\`, default *Retail Orders*) opens with its checks, a run detail opens.
- **\`backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py\`** — \`DATAQ_BEARER\` → \`Authorization\` header, so the 12-check API smoke runs against the deployed frontend proxy (\`DATAQ_API=https://<frontend-host>\`) with a real AAD token.
- **Runbook** — live-smoke section: both automated lanes + the manual mutating checklist (live run, trigger correlation, alert delivery, Claude Desktop 4-query MCP smoke).
- **\`.gitignore\`** — \`frontend/e2e-live/.auth/\` (the captured session is bearer material).

## Test plan
- Local lane unchanged: \`pnpm exec playwright test e2e/\` → **17 passed**.
- Live lane wiring: \`E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL=… playwright test --list\` → 3 live-smoke specs, no webServer.
- \`pnpm lint\` / prettier / black / ruff / mypy / \`mkdocs build --strict\` all green.
- Actual live execution against \`dataq-app-frontend.…azurecontainerapps.io\` is the follow-up verification step (needs an interactive sign-in).

attachment.snippet:
1	# Browser E2E (Playwright)
2	
3	Drives the **real app in a browser** under dev-bypass auth against the seeded
4	demo dataset — the other half of the full-stack smoke (#128). The httpx API
5	smoke (`backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py`) proves `HTTP → service → DB`; this proves
6	the React UI a user actually clicks: `browser → Vite proxy → api → DB`.
7	
8	> Live `test()`/runs against real Snowflake/S3/etc. stay out of scope (no creds
9	> locally — the documented deferred smoke). The connectivity tests here
10	> **fail-soft**; the specs assert the health/error path renders, not that a real
11	> datasource is reachable.
12	
13	## Specs
14	
15	| Spec                       | Covers                                                                                       |
16	| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
17	| `smoke.spec.ts`            | dev-bypass auth, app shell, sider nav                                                        |
18	| `connections.spec.ts`      | seeded connections grouped by type, "Test all" health path                                   |
19	| `suites.spec.ts`           | seeded suite → checks; check + suite authoring round-trips                                   |
20	| `results.spec.ts`          | seeded runs, run-detail drill-down, pipeline-runs feed                                       |
21	| `schedules.spec.ts`        | SchedulesPanel: add / pause / delete + invalid-cron 422 path                                 |
22	| `trigger-bindings.spec.ts` | TriggersPanel: bind pipeline / disable / remove (seeded ADF connection)                      |
23	| `notifications.spec.ts`    | NotificationsPanel: threshold routing persisted across reload; write-only webhook affordance |
24	
25	antd Select gotchas (learned per spec, reuse these): rc-select pre-highlights
26	option 0 when nothing is selected, so `Enter` alone takes the first option and
27	`ArrowDown`+`Enter` takes the **second**; option nodes live in an off-viewport
28	rc-virtual-list measurement container, so `getByRole('option').click()` is
29	flaky — prefer keyboard navigation with the arrow-delta computed from the
30	currently rendered value (see `notifications.spec.ts`).
31	
32	## Run it locally
33	
34	1. Bring up the stack and seed the demo data:
35	
36	   ```bash
37	   docker compose up -d            # postgres + redis + api + worker + frontend
38	   conda run -n dataq python -m backend.scripts.seed_dev
39	   ```
40	
41	2. Install browsers once, then run:
42	
43	   ```bash
44	   cd frontend
45	   pnpm install
46	   pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium
47	   pnpm e2e            # headless
48	   pnpm e2e:ui         # Playwright UI mode (watch/debug)
49	   ```
50	
51	The config (`playwright.config.ts`) targets `E2E_BASE_URL` (default
52	`http://localhost:3000`) and **reuses** the already-running compose/`pnpm dev`
53	server on :3000. Point it elsewhere with `E2E_BASE_URL=… pnpm e2e`.
54	
55	## In CI
56	
57	The `frontend-e2e` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` spins up Postgres + Redis,
58	pip-installs the backend, runs migrations, seeds the demo data, launches
59	`uvicorn` on :8000, then lets Playwright start its own Vite dev server on :3000
60	(`VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET=http://localhost:8000`) and run the specs. No Azure /
61	real datasource credentials are involved — dev-bypass only.

7	- DB migrations are backward-compatible; the migrate job runs `alembic upgrade head`
8	  **before** the app rolls.
9	- Deploy via the **Deploy** workflow (`workflow_dispatch`); verify `/healthz` 200,
10	  `/api/v1/me` 401 (auth enforced), SPA + deep links load.
11	- Docs site published (this site) and linked from the README.
12	
13	Full deploy steps + verification: **[deploy/README.md](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/blob/main/deploy/README.md)**.
14	
15	## Known limitations (v1)
16	
17	- **GX-only** check engine (Databricks DQX deferred to v1.1); batch-oriented (not
18	  streaming).
19	- **Single tenant**, suite-level access sharing; workspace-admin is a config allowlist.
20	- Interactive **datasource browsing** (container browser, 3-level UC catalog picker) is
21	  deferred — you specify targets explicitly. JSON flat files deferred (CSV/Parquet in v1).
22	- Auth is **Azure AD only** (no API keys / service tokens yet — see ADR 0026 / #461).

1	"""Full-stack API E2E smoke against a running DataQ stack (no mocks).
2	
3	Drives the **real** HTTP API the way the frontend does — dev-bypass auth, the
4	seeded demo dataset — and asserts the read + authoring paths work end-to-end
5	(HTTP → service → Postgres). Run it after `docker compose up` + the seed:
6	
7	    python -m backend.scripts.e2e_smoke                 # http://localhost:8000
8	    DATAQ_API=http://localhost:8000 python -m backend.scripts.e2e_smoke
9	
10	What it verifies (exit 0 = all passed):
11	  1. the six seeded connection types are listed (secrets never returned);
12	  2. the demo suites + their checks are retrievable;
13	  3. an authoring round-trip: create suite → add a check → read back → delete;
14	  4. a dry-run *attempt* returns a structured result/error, not a crash
15	     (live connectivity fails-soft without real datasource creds — expected).
16	
17	Live `test()`/runs against real Snowflake/S3/etc. are out of scope (no creds —
18	the documented deferred smoke); this proves the app layer, not the datasources.
19	"""
20	
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import os
24	import sys
25	import uuid
26	from typing import Any
27	
28	import httpx
29	
30	API = os.environ.get("DATAQ_API", "http://localhost:8000")
31	BASE = f"{API}/api/v1"
32	
33	_passed = 0
34	_failed = 0
35	
36	
37	def check(label: str, ok: bool, detail: str = "") -> None:
38	    global _passed, _failed
39	    mark = "PASS" if ok else "FAIL"
40	    if ok:
41	        _passed += 1
42	    else:
43	        _failed += 1
44	    print(f"[{mark}] {label}" + (f" — {detail}" if detail else ""))
45	
46	
47	def main() -> int:
48	    # Dev-bypass: no bearer needed when AUTH_DEV_BYPASS=true + environment=dev.
49	    client = httpx.Client(base_url=BASE, timeout=30.0)
50	
51	    # 1. Connections — six types present, no secret leaked.
52	    r = client.get("/connections")
53	    conns: list[dict[str, Any]] = r.json() if r.status_code == 200 else []
54	    types = {c["type"] for c in conns}
55	    expected_types = {"snowflake", "s3", "adls_gen2", "unity_catalog", "adf", "airflow"}
56	    check("GET /connections returns 200", r.status_code == 200, f"status={r.status_code}")
57	    check(
58	        "all six connection types seeded",
59	        expected_types.issubset(types),
60	        f"missing={expected_types - types}",
61	    )
62	    check(
63	        "secret is never returned on a connection",
64	        all("secret" not in c and "secret_ref" not in c for c in conns),
65	        "a connection payload leaked a secret field",
66	    )
67	
68	    # 2. Suites + checks retrievable.
69	    r = client.get("/suites")
70	    suites: list[dict[str, Any]] = r.json() if r.status_code == 200 else []
71	    check("GET /suites returns the demo suites", len(suites) >= 3, f"count={len(suites)}")
72	    orders = next((s for s in suites if s["name"] == "Orders quality"), None)
73	    check("'Orders quality' suite present", orders is not None)
74	    if orders is not None:
75	        r = client.get(f"/suites/{orders['id']}/checks")
76	        checks = r.json() if r.status_code == 200 else []
77	        names = {c["name"] for c in checks}
78	        check(
79	            "'Orders quality' has its seeded checks",
80	            {"order_id not null", "amount in range"}.issubset(names),
81	            f"got={sorted(names)}",
82	        )
83	        amount = next((c for c in checks if c["name"] == "amount in range"), None)
84	        check(
85	            "severity thresholds round-trip on a check",
86	            amount is not None and amount["fail_threshold"] == 5,
87	            f"check={amount}",
88	        )
89	
90	    # 3. Authoring round-trip: create suite → add check → read → delete.
91	    # Bind to a *datasource* connection (Snowflake) — suites target datasources,
92	    # not orchestration providers (ADF/Airflow); this also routes the dry-run
93	    # below through the real Snowflake path instead of an unsupported-type 422.
94	    conn_id = next((c["id"] for c in conns if c["type"] == "snowflake"), None)
95	    if conn_id is not None:
96	        name = f"e2e-smoke-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}"
97	        r = client.post("/suites", json={"name": name, "connection_id": conn_id})
98	        check("POST /suites creates a suite", r.status_code == 201, f"status={r.status_code}")
99	        sid = r.json()["id"] if r.status_code == 201 else None
100	        if sid:
101	            r = client.post(
102	                f"/suites/{sid}/checks",
103	                json={
104	                    "name": "smoke not null",
105	                    "expectation_type": "expect_column_values_to_not_be_null",
106	                    "config": {"column": "id"},
107	                },
108	            )
109	            check("POST a check into the suite", r.status_code == 201, f"status={r.status_code}")
110	            r = client.get(f"/suites/{sid}/checks")
111	            check("the new check reads back", r.status_code == 200 and len(r.json()) == 1)
112	
113	            # 4. Dry-run against the real Snowflake path: with no live creds it
114	            # fails soft as a structured 502, not a 500 crash. (200 if creds were
115	            # somehow present; a 422 here would mean the type was wrongly rejected.)
116	            r = client.post(
117	                f"/suites/{sid}/checks/dryrun",
118	                json={
119	                    "expectation_type": "expect_column_values_to_not_be_null",
120	                    "config": {"column": "id"},
121	                    "table": "ORDERS",
122	                    "schema": "PUBLIC",
123	                },
124	            )
125	            check(
126	                "dry-run on the Snowflake path fails soft (502 structured, no crash)",
127	                r.status_code in (200, 502),
128	                f"status={r.status_code}",
129	            )
130	
131	            r = client.delete(f"/suites/{sid}")
132	            check(
133	                "DELETE the smoke suite cleans up", r.status_code == 204, f"status={r.status_code}"
134	            )
135	
136	    print(f"\n{_passed} passed, {_failed} failed")
137	    return 1 if _failed else 0
138	
139	
140	if __name__ == "__main__":
141	    sys.exit(main())

7	//
8	// It assumes a running stack reachable at E2E_BASE_URL (default the compose
9	// frontend on :3000, whose Vite proxy forwards /api → the api service). Bring it
10	// up first: `docker compose up` + `python -m backend.scripts.seed_dev`.
11	//
12	// Locally the `webServer` block reuses that already-running :3000 dev server. In
13	// CI it starts its own `pnpm dev` (the backend is launched by the workflow step
14	// before Playwright runs). See frontend/e2e/README.md.
15	const baseURL = process.env.E2E_BASE_URL || 'http://localhost:3000';
16	
17	export default defineConfig({
18	  testDir: './e2e',
19	  fullyParallel: true,
20	  // Fail the build if a `test.only` is committed; flaky-retry only in CI.
21	  forbidOnly: !!process.env.CI,
22	  retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0,
23	  workers: process.env.CI ? 1 : undefined,
24	  reporter: process.env.CI ? [['list'], ['html', { open: 'never' }]] : 'list',
25	  timeout: 30_000,
26	  expect: { timeout: 10_000 },
27	  use: {
28	    baseURL,
29	    trace: 'on-first-retry',
30	    screenshot: 'only-on-failure',
31	  },
32	  projects: [{ name: 'chromium', use: { ...devices['Desktop Chrome'] } }],
33	  webServer: {
34	    command: 'pnpm dev --host --port 3000',
35	    url: baseURL,
36	    // Locally: reuse the compose/`pnpm dev` server already on :3000. In CI:
37	    // start a fresh one (the api is already up on :8000 from a prior step).
38	    reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI,
39	    timeout: 120_000,
40	    env: {
41	      VITE_AUTH_DEV_BYPASS: 'true',
42	      VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET: process.env.VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET || 'http://localhost:8000',
43	    },
44	  },
45	});

# Feature matrix

One-page reference: what runs where. For the concepts behind the columns, see
[Concepts](concepts.md) and [Datasources & checks](datasources-checks.md).

## Check kinds × datasources

| Check kind | Snowflake | Unity Catalog | ADLS Gen2 (files) | S3 (files) |
|---|:-:|:-:|:-:|:-:|
| GX expectations (column / table shape) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Custom SQL (rows returned = failures) | ✅ | ✅ | — | — |
| Freshness monitor (hours since latest timestamp) | ✅ | ✅ | — | — |
| Volume monitor (row count in range) | ✅ | ✅ | — | — |
| Column profiler (nulls, distinct, min/max, top values) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Dry-run preview | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

Custom SQL and the freshness/volume monitors run a SQL query, so they're **SQL-datasource
only** (flat-file support is a tracked enhancement,
[#520](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/520)). Flat-file suites target a
file or a batch pattern (e.g. `orders_*.csv`) in CSV or Parquet.

## Ways a suite runs

| Mode | Where | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Run now | Suite detail → Run panel | Live per-check progress + cancel |
| Cron schedule | Suite detail → Schedules | 5-field cron, IANA timezone, DST-aware, [no backfill](scheduling.md) |
| Pipeline trigger | Suite detail → Triggers | Runs on a pipeline/DAG **success** — ADF + Airflow, see [Orchestration](orchestration.md) |
| API / MCP | `POST /suites/{id}/run` · `trigger_suite_run` MCP tool | Same authz as the UI |

## Severity & results

| Capability | Notes |
|---|---|
| Severity tiers | warn / fail / critical, banded from the observed unexpected-% (ADR 0005/0016) |
| Operational statuses | `error` (evaluation threw) and `skip` (precondition unmet) are distinct from failures |
| Health score | Severity-weighted, on the Dashboard |
| Failing-row samples | Redacted column-aware before display (suite column policy + classifier) |
| Run history retention | Samples purged after the retention window; metric trends kept |

## Alerting

| Capability | Notes |
|---|---|
| Channels | Teams (workspace + per-suite webhook), Slack, email — [details](notifications.md) |
| Threshold | Per suite: fail-only / warn+ (default) / always |
| Routing | Severity-aware urgency; critical escalates |
| Dedup | First failure / escalation only; clean run resets |
| Snooze | Per check, N hours |

## Orchestration providers (not datasources)

| Provider | Failure detection | Trigger on success |
|---|---|---|
| Azure Data Factory | Azure Monitor alert → webhook (+10-min poll) | ✅ trigger bindings |
| Apache Airflow | DAG callback → HMAC webhook (+10-min poll) | ✅ trigger bindings |

## Interfaces

| Surface | What |
|---|---|
| Web UI | Dashboard · Connections · Suites · Results · Profile · Admin · Settings |
| REST API | Versioned `/api/v1` (Swagger in non-prod) |
| MCP | 8 curated tools at `/mcp` for AI assistants (ADR 0008) |

import io
p = 'README.md'
s = open(p).read()
s = s.replace("column profiler and dry-run preview. [Feature matrix", "column profiler on every datasource (dry-run preview on Snowflake). [Feature matrix")
open(p, 'w').write(s)
p = 'docs/notifications.md'
s = open(p).read()
s = s.replace("""Webhook URLs are validated against an allow-list of hosts (`webhook.office.com`,
`hooks.slack.com`, …) so a typo can't exfiltrate alerts to an arbitrary endpoint.""",
"""Webhook URLs are validated against a **per-channel** host allow-list (Teams:
`webhook.office.com` / `logic.azure.com`; Slack: `hooks.slack.com`) so a typo can't
exfiltrate alerts to an arbitrary endpoint. Only Teams has a per-suite override; Slack
and email are workspace-wide.""")
open(p, 'w').write(s)
print("ok")
EOF

- feature-matrix: dry-run row corrected to Snowflake-only (dryrun_service
  _SUPPORTED_TYPES = {snowflake}; UC/ADLS/S3 422 today) with a pointer to the
  newly filed #532 (extend dry-run via the runner dispatch).

  all-datasource one).

  only Teams has a per-suite override.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <noreply@anthropic.com>" && git push -q && gh pr comment 528 --body "## Docs-accuracy review — verified + fixed

A docs-consistency agent verified every factual claim against the code. One real overclaim found and fixed in the fixup commit:

- **Dry-run preview is Snowflake-only in v1** (\`dryrun_service._SUPPORTED_TYPES = {\"snowflake\"}\` — UC/ADLS/S3 get a 422). The feature-matrix row and the README bullet claimed all four datasources; both corrected, and the gap is now tracked as #532 (route dry-run through the same runner dispatch as real runs).
- Polish: the webhook allow-lists are **per-channel** (Teams: \`webhook.office.com\`/\`logic.azure.com\`; Slack: \`hooks.slack.com\`), and only Teams has a per-suite override.

Everything else checked out exactly against code: notification env vars + \`alert_on\` default + write-only webhook + routing/dedup/suppression semantics, scheduling (cron validation, DST, no-backfill, 60s tick, multi-schedule), SQL-only category gating (#520), MCP tool count, check-editor field labels, the \`#troubleshooting\` anchor, and the nav (all pages present or deliberately excluded; strict build green)." 2>&1 | tail -1

input.prompt:
Code-review branch test/e2e-live-smoke-lane vs main in /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ (`git diff main...test/e2e-live-smoke-lane`). It adds an opt-in Playwright live-smoke lane: conditional playwright.config.ts (E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL flips testDir/webServer/project), frontend/e2e-live/{global-setup.ts,live-test.ts,smoke.spec.ts}, DATAQ_BEARER support in backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py, .gitignore entry for e2e-live/.auth/, and docs (frontend/e2e/README.md + docs/runbook-faq.md).

Focus:
1. Security: the captured sessionStorage file contains AAD tokens — is the gitignore airtight (path correct relative to repo root)? Any way the session JSON leaks (trace files? playwright report? console)? Note traces are on-first-retry and screenshots only-on-failure — do those artifacts land somewhere committed?
2. Config correctness: with E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL set, does anything still reference the local webServer/env? forbidOnly/retries interplay; globalSetup path resolution under ESM (they hit a __dirname issue already — verify the fix `path.resolve('e2e-live', ...)` is cwd-safe for both `pnpm e2e` from frontend/ and playwright invoked from repo root).
3. live-test.ts fixture: does overriding `context` this way actually apply addInitScript before the page loads? Any issue with JSON.parse of the session file when global-setup skipped capture (stale-file reuse) vs missing file?
4. e2e_smoke.py: header wiring, and whether any of its 12 checks would misbehave against the live deployment (e.g. expected connection types vs the 5 live prod connections — snowflake/uc/adls/adf/airflow, NO s3 live per the memory note — would 'all six connection types seeded' FAIL against prod? If so that's a real finding: the script's live-mode docstring oversells).
5. The docs instructions: do the commands actually work as written (pnpm e2e passes E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL through? az CLI token command shape)?

Return up to 6 findings: file, line, summary, concrete failure scenario. If none, say none.

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Review complete. I read the full diff and verified every selector against the actual components (`Dashboard.tsx`, `Admin.tsx`, `Settings.tsx`, `CheckNew.tsx`, `checkFormFields.tsx`, `expectationCatalog.ts`, `Suites.tsx`, `RunDetail.tsx`, `SimpleList.tsx`), the seed shape against `Result.sample_failures` / `redact_sample_failures` / the `SampleFailures` component, and the toolchain behavior (Playwright 1.61 name-matching semantics, antd 6.5 expand-icon aria-label, monaco 0.55 SQL auto-closing pairs).

## Findings (4)

**1. Seed idempotency gap: already-seeded databases never get `sample_failures`, so the new results.spec test fails on any pre-existing stack** — `backend/scripts/demo_data.py`, `_seed_runs` (~line 445 on the branch).
The new sample rides `_SEED_RUN_RESULTS`, but `_seed_runs` skips the whole run when the `seed:run:succeeded` marker already exists. Concrete failure: any dev compose DB (or the harness/prod-demo DB) seeded before this branch keeps the old "status in set" result with `sample_failures = NULL`; re-running `seed_dev` is a documented no-op, the row still expands (the check-trend makes `rowExpandable` true) but `getByText('unknwon')` times out. CI passes (fresh Postgres service) — this bites exactly the local loop the config header tells people to use (`reuseExistingServer` + compose). Fix: an idempotent backfill (`UPDATE` the seeded fail result's `sample_failures` when NULL) alongside the marker guard, or at minimum call the reseed requirement out.

**2. admin.spec's `'Suites'` section assertion is vacuous — it's satisfied by the sidebar nav link** — `frontend/e2e/admin.spec.ts:13-15`.
`page.getByText('Suites', { exact: true }).first()` matches the always-present nav item (`App.tsx:56` renders `<Link to="/suites">Suites</Link>` on every page), so if the Admin KPI card row broke or `AdminOverview` failed to render its metric cards, this loop iteration still passes ('Members' / 'Access grants' are genuine). Scope it to the content area, e.g. `page.locator('.ant-card').filter({ hasText: 'Suites' })` or assert the MetricCard the way dashboard.spec does.

**3. `${WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS:-dev-bypass@dataq.local}` can't be opted out, and running stacks won't pick it up** — `docker-compose.yml:86-88`.
Two operational edges of the same line: (a) `:-` substitutes the default for *empty* as well as unset, so `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS=""` in `.env` cannot restore the non-admin dev identity (needed to manually exercise the `Forbidden` gate on `/admin`/`/settings` that #289's frontend ships) — `${WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS-dev-bypass@dataq.local}` (no colon) preserves the escape hatch; (b) env is applied at container create (the known ACA/compose snapshot behavior), so an already-running api container stays non-admin and `admin.spec.ts` 403s locally until `docker compose up -d api` recreates it. Worth a line in `e2e/README.md` given finding 1 has the same "re-provision your stack" remedy.

**4. dashboard.spec test 2 passes even when the summary endpoint returns nothing/errors** — `frontend/e2e/dashboard.spec.ts:24-29`.
`QualityTrends`/`SuitePerformance` render their Card titles unconditionally (`Dashboard.tsx` passes `summary?.trend ?? []`, and on fetch error the KPI cards still mount with em-dashes), so the two `getByText(/Quality Trends|Trends/i)`-style assertions can't fail for a broken data path — only the `RecentRuns` 'Orders quality' assertion is data-backed (and in test 1, only the Total Runs `/^\d+$/` assertion is). If the intent is "widgets render *seeded* data", assert chart content (e.g. a recharts `svg`/point inside the trend card) or the per-suite bar for 'Orders quality'.

## Checked, no issue

- **6-tuple widening**: all 8 entries across both lists are 6-wide, the unpack and `Result(sample_failures=...)` kwarg match the model column (`models.py:368`), and the seeded shape (`unexpected_count`/`unexpected_percent`/scalar `partial_unexpected_list`) is exactly what `redact_sample_failures` passes through (tested column `status` is not name/value-sensitive per `column_classification.py`, suite has no `column_policy`) and what `SampleFailures` renders (scalars → `{value}` rows).
- **Monaco**: monaco 0.55's SQL language does auto-close `{`, but the default `autoClosingOvertype: 'auto'` types over the auto-inserted `}` during char-by-char `keyboard.type`, so `{batch}` lands literally; the query passes both the frontend and backend read-only validators.
- **Selectors**: `getByRole` name-matching is substring/case-insensitive in Playwright 1.61 (so `'Dashboard'` matches the actual "Monitoring Dashboard" h3 — intentional or not, it works); antd 6.5 ships `aria-label="Expand row"`; `SimpleList` renders `role="listitem"`; `Fail ≥`/`Timestamp column`/`Minimum rows` labels, the `Webhooks` tab, both provider Tags, and the readonly URL input (`admin_service.webhook_configs` always emits both rows for the seeded connections, token configured or not) all exist as asserted; `monitor:freshness`/`monitor:volume` render as the check's `expectation_type` in the suite detail list.
- **Parallelism**: CI runs `workers: 1`; locally the three check-editor tests + suites.spec all mutate 'Orders quality' concurrently but every create/verify/delete is scoped to a `Date.now()`-unique name with distinct prefixes, so no cross-test assertion can observe another's rows.
- **Admin env vs existing tests**: the CI var is scoped to the `frontend-e2e` job only; backend tests control `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` via the `conftest.py` monkeypatch fixture, and no pre-existing e2e spec references admin surfaces or asserts nav contents. All seeded suites are owned by the dev-bypass user, so admin's workspace-wide visibility changes nothing the other specs see.</result>

s = open(p).read()
s = s.replace(
"""    // Playwright specs live in e2e/ and run under `pnpm e2e`, not Vitest — they
    // import @playwright/test and drive a browser, so keep Vitest out of them.
    exclude: [...configDefaults.exclude, 'e2e/**'],""",
"""    // Playwright specs live in e2e/ + e2e-live/ and run under `pnpm e2e`, not
    // Vitest — they import @playwright/test and drive a browser, so keep Vitest
    // out of them.
    exclude: [...configDefaults.exclude, 'e2e/**', 'e2e-live/**'],""")
open(p, 'w').write(s)
print("patched")
EOF
cd frontend && pnpm test 2>&1 | grep -E "Test Files|Tests " | tail -2

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Browser E2E (Playwright)

Drives the **real app in a browser** under dev-bypass auth against the seeded
demo dataset — the other half of the full-stack smoke (#128). The httpx API
smoke (`backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py`) proves `HTTP → service → DB`; this proves
the React UI a user actually clicks: `browser → Vite proxy → api → DB`.

> Live `test()`/runs against real Snowflake/S3/etc. stay out of scope (no creds
> locally — the documented deferred smoke). The connectivity tests here
> **fail-soft**; the specs assert the health/error path renders, not that a real
> datasource is reachable.

## Specs

| Spec                       | Covers                                                                                       |
| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `smoke.spec.ts`            | dev-bypass auth, app shell, sider nav                                                        |
| `connections.spec.ts`      | seeded connections grouped by type, "Test all" health path                                   |
| `suites.spec.ts`           | seeded suite → checks; check + suite authoring round-trips                                   |
| `results.spec.ts`          | seeded runs, run-detail drill-down, pipeline-runs feed                                       |
| `schedules.spec.ts`        | SchedulesPanel: add / pause / delete + invalid-cron 422 path                                 |
| `trigger-bindings.spec.ts` | TriggersPanel: bind pipeline / disable / remove (seeded ADF connection)                      |
| `notifications.spec.ts`    | NotificationsPanel: threshold routing persisted across reload; write-only webhook affordance |

antd Select gotchas (learned per spec, reuse these): rc-select pre-highlights
option 0 when nothing is selected, so `Enter` alone takes the first option and
`ArrowDown`+`Enter` takes the **second**; option nodes live in an off-viewport
rc-virtual-list measurement container, so `getByRole('option').click()` is
flaky — prefer keyboard navigation with the arrow-delta computed from the
currently rendered value (see `notifications.spec.ts`).

## Run it locally

1. Bring up the stack and seed the demo data:

   ```bash
   docker compose up -d            # postgres + redis + api + worker + frontend
   conda run -n dataq python -m backend.scripts.seed_dev
   ```

2. Install browsers once, then run:

   ```bash
   cd frontend
   pnpm install
   pnpm exec playwright install --with-deps chromium
   pnpm e2e            # headless
   pnpm e2e:ui         # Playwright UI mode (watch/debug)
   ```

The config (`playwright.config.ts`) targets `E2E_BASE_URL` (default
`http://localhost:3000`) and **reuses** the already-running compose/`pnpm dev`
server on :3000. Point it elsewhere with `E2E_BASE_URL=… pnpm e2e`.

## In CI

The `frontend-e2e` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` spins up Postgres + Redis,
pip-installs the backend, runs migrations, seeds the demo data, launches
`uvicorn` on :8000, then lets Playwright start its own Vite dev server on :3000
(`VITE_API_PROXY_TARGET=http://localhost:8000`) and run the specs. No Azure /
real datasource credentials are involved — dev-bypass only.

+> then re-run the seed (it's idempotent and backfills what it can).

<task-id>a342178a66b00e978</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_013XJKqKQSKBSYJDB3GLEexT</tool-use-id>

<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Review PR 531 live-smoke lane" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Review complete. I verified the diff against the frontend auth code (`authClient.ts` does use sessionStorage via `WebStorageStateStore`), the routes (`/dashboard`, `/suites/:id`, `/results/:runId` all exist with `level={3}` headings), the local demo seed, and the Terraform SSO app registrations.

## Findings

**1. HIGH — `frontend/e2e-live/global-setup.ts:17` — `SESSION_FILE` is cwd-relative, so the AAD-token file can escape the gitignore.**
`path.resolve('e2e-live', '.auth', 'session.json')` resolves against `process.cwd()`, not the config dir. Playwright resolves `testDir`/`globalSetup` relative to the config file but never chdirs. Failure scenario: `npx playwright test -c frontend/playwright.config.ts` (or any repo-root invocation) runs fine — config paths resolve — but global-setup writes the captured AAD tokens to `<repo>/e2e-live/.auth/session.json`, which the gitignore entry `frontend/e2e-live/.auth/` does **not** cover; a subsequent `git add -A` commits live bearer tokens. It also silently defeats stale-file reuse (each cwd gets its own file → forced re-login). Fix: anchor to the module — `path.join(path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url)), '.auth', 'session.json')` (or `import.meta.dirname`, Node ≥20.11). The gitignore path itself is correct for the *intended* location; the bug is that the writer isn't pinned to it.

**2. MED — `backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py:55, 71–87` — against the live deployment, 5+ of the 12 checks FAIL; the new live-mode docstring materially oversells.**
Line 55 `expected_types` includes `"s3"`, but prod has 5 live connections (snowflake/uc/adls/adf/airflow — no s3) → "all six connection types seeded" fails. Worse: lines 71–87 expect ≥3 suites, a suite named **"Orders quality"** with checks "order_id not null"/"amount in range" and `fail_threshold == 5` — those exist only in the local `demo_data.py` seed (line 127); the prod workspace carries **"Retail Orders"**. So the documented `DATAQ_API=… DATAQ_BEARER=… python -m backend.scripts.e2e_smoke` exits 1 on a perfectly healthy deployment. The docstring's caveat ("the connection-type assertion expects the demo/harness connection set") covers only 1 of the ~5 seed-coupled checks. Fix: gate the seed assertions (e.g., skip when `DATAQ_BEARER` is set, or an explicit `DATAQ_EXPECT_SEED=1`).

**3. MED — az CLI token command in `e2e_smoke.py` docstring (and referenced from `docs/runbook-faq.md`) won't mint a token as written.**
`az account get-access-token --resource api://<api-app-id>` requires the Azure CLI first-party client (`04b07795-8ddb-461a-bbee-02f9e1bf7b46`) to have consent for the API. `deploy/terraform/azure/sso.tf:94` pre-authorizes **only the SPA** on the API scope. Failure scenario: the command returns `AADSTS65001: The user or administrator has not consented…` — the entire API-level live-smoke lane is un-runnable per the docs. Fix: add a second `azuread_application_pre_authorized` for the Azure CLI client id (or document extracting the SPA's token / a device-code flow instead).

**4. LOW — `frontend/playwright.config.ts:29–31, 38–39` — token-in-trace protection is only "CI is never set", not structural.**
In live mode `retries` is `process.env.CI ? 2 : 0` and `trace: 'on-first-retry'` is kept. If someone runs the live lane with `CI` exported (direnv/mise, or copy-pasting a CI invocation), a flaky first attempt records a full trace containing every `Authorization: Bearer` request header **and** the injected init-script argument (the entire serialized sessionStorage, i.e. the OIDC user blob). The artifacts themselves land safely — `outputDir`/HTML report resolve relative to the config file, so they hit the gitignored `frontend/test-results/` / `frontend/playwright-report/` even from repo root, and the live reporter is `list`-only — but the trace zip is a plaintext-token artifact people routinely share/upload. Hardening: in the `liveBaseURL` branch force `retries: 0` and `trace: 'off'`.

**5. LOW — `frontend/e2e-live/global-setup.ts:25–30` + `live-test.ts:14` — stale-file reuse isn't keyed to URL or account.**
Freshness is mtime-only (<40 min). Failure scenario: capture a session against deployment A, then within 40 min run with `E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL` pointing at deployment B (or wanting a different test user) — global-setup silently reuses A's file, the fixture injects wrong-origin/wrong-audience tokens, the SPA falls back to the sign-in screen, and all three specs die as opaque heading-visibility timeouts rather than a clear "session mismatch" error. Storing `{ baseURL, capturedAt, entries }` in the JSON and re-capturing on mismatch would fix both this and make the parse shape explicit. (The pure missing-file `JSON.parse` worry is a non-issue: `globalSetup` always runs before the fixture in this lane and creates the file, or throws.)

**6. LOW — `backend/scripts/e2e_smoke.py:97–134` — in live mode the authoring round-trip can orphan a suite in the prod workspace.**
No try/finally around the create→check→dryrun→delete sequence: any unhandled exception mid-way (httpx transport error, non-JSON 502 body from the nginx proxy making `r.json()` raise) skips the `DELETE`, leaving an `e2e-smoke-<hex>` suite bound to the **live** Snowflake connection visible to workspace users. Local-mode this was cosmetic; against prod it's clutter with a real connection attached. Wrap the tail in try/finally keyed on `sid`.

**Verified clean (questions you raised that check out):** the live config branch fully drops the local `webServer` and `VITE_*` env (nothing else references :3000/:8000); `globalSetup: './e2e-live/global-setup.ts'` and `testDir` resolve relative to the config file, so they're repo-root-safe (only `SESSION_FILE` isn't — finding 1); the `context` fixture override is correct — the built-in `page` fixture depends on `context`, so `addInitScript` is registered before any page/document exists and the sessionStorage lands before app code runs; `E2E_LIVE_BASE_URL=… pnpm e2e` passes env through fine (`e2e: "playwright test"`, no hardcoded `--project`); default `E2E_LIVE_SUITE` "Retail Orders" matches the live workspace; and the spec selectors match the real routes/headings (`Typography.Title level={3}`, `/results/:runId`).</result>

</task-notification>

Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".

## claude.ai Context7
Use this server to fetch current documentation whenever the user asks about a library, framework, SDK, API, CLI tool, or cloud service -- even well-known ones like React, Next.js, Prisma, Express, Tailwind, Django, or Spring Boot. This includes API syntax, configuration, version migration, library-specific debugging, setup instructions, and CLI tool usage. Use even when you think you know the answer -- your training data may not reflect recent changes. Prefer this over web search for library docs.

Do not use for: refactoring, writing scripts from scratch, debugging business logic, code review, or general programming concepts.

## claude.ai Hugging Face
You have tools for using the Hugging Face Hub. arXiv paper id's are often used as references between datasets, models and papers. There are over 100 tags in use, common tags include 'Text Generation', 'Transformers', 'Image Classification' and so on.
Hugging Face tools are being used by authenticated user 'TheurgicDuke771'

## claude.ai Microsoft Learn
# Microsoft Learn MCP Server

This server gives structured access to official Microsoft and Azure documentation via three tools:

## Tools

### microsoft_docs_search
Search official documentation and return up to 10 concise, high-quality content chunks (max 500 tokens each), including title, URL, and excerpt.

- Use first to get a quick, reliable overview
- Ideal for grounding answers in Microsoft knowledge

### microsoft_code_sample_search
Search for code snippets and examples in official Microsoft Learn documentation and return up to 20 relevant, high-quality code samples.

- Use when you need to provide sample Microsoft/Azure related code in your answers.  
- Ideal for generating code snippets or practical implementation examples.  
- Optional parameter `language` can filter results. 

### microsoft_docs_fetch
Fetch and convert full Microsoft documentation pages to markdown.

- Use after search when you need full content from a specific URL
- Required for detailed tutorials, troubleshooting, prerequisites, code samples, or when search results are incomplete or outdated

## Workflow

1. Use `microsoft_docs_search` to find relevant documents.
2. If you need code examples or practical snippets, use `microsoft_code_sample_search`.
3. If deeper or complete information is needed, use `microsoft_docs_fetch`.

**Search gives breadth. Code Sample Search gives practical examples. Fetch gives depth.**

All content comes from Microsoft Learn or official sources, returned in clean markdown format.

## claude.ai Postman
Read the instructions resource completely for detailed usage instructions before answering any API-related questions.

## context7
Use this server to retrieve up-to-date documentation and code examples for any library.

content.content: Launching skill: code-review

content.text:
Review target: `530`

`low effort → 1 diff pass → no verify → ≤4 findings`

## Turn 1 — read

One tool call: read the unified diff (`git diff @{upstream}...HEAD; git diff HEAD`
to cover both committed and uncommitted changes, or `git diff main...HEAD` /
the target passed as an argument). Skip test/fixture
hunks (`test/`, `spec/`, `__tests__/`, `*_test.*`, `*.test.*`,
`fixtures/`, `testdata/`) — test-file changes are not reviewed at this level.
No subagents, no full-file reads.

## Turn 2 — findings

Flag runtime-correctness bugs visible from the hunk alone: inverted/wrong
condition, off-by-one, null/undefined deref where adjacent lines show the value
can be absent, removed guard, falsy-zero check, missing `await`,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in a catch that should propagate.
Also flag — still from the hunk alone — new code that duplicates an existing
helper visible in the diff context, and dead code the diff leaves behind.

Do **not** flag style, naming, perf, missing tests, or anything outside the
hunk.

Output at most **4 findings**, most-severe first, one line each:
`path/to/file.ext:123 — what's wrong and the concrete failure`. If nothing
qualifies, output exactly `(none)`.

diff --git a/context/post-v1-roadmap.md b/context/post-v1-roadmap.md
index d927f9cd..30bc7d2e 100644
--- a/context/post-v1-roadmap.md
+++ b/context/post-v1-roadmap.md
@@ -219,6 +219,51 @@ palette** (the shipped indigo theme stands).
 
 ---
 
+## Theme 13 — MCP tool expansion (candidate endpoints)
+
+The v1 MCP server exposes 8 curated tools (ADR [0008](../docs/adr/0008-mcp-server.md));
+the REST surface has ~52 more endpoints. Candidates below, tiered by risk — every new
+tool stays a thin service-layer wrapper with `require_permission` authz + sample
+redaction, exactly like the existing 8 (`backend/app/mcp/server.py`). Cross-cutting
+dependencies: **#488** (workspace-admin visibility in MCP tools) and **#461 / ADR 0026**
+(DataQ-issued API keys, which unblock headless MCP clients).
+
+**Tier 1 — high-value safe reads:**
+
+| Candidate tool | Wraps | NL query it serves |
+|---|---|---|
+| `list_checks` / `get_check` | `GET /suites/{id}/checks[/{id}]` | "what checks does the orders suite have?" |
+| `get_check_history` | `GET .../checks/{id}/history` | "how has null-rate trended this week?" |
+| `list_runs` | `GET /runs` (filterable) | "show me yesterday's failed runs" |
+| `get_run_results` | `GET /runs/{id}` (redacted) | "why did run X fail?" |
+| `list_connections` | `GET /connections` — names/types/health **only, never config or secrets** | "which connections are unhealthy?" |
+| `list_schedules` | `GET /schedules` | "when does the orders suite run?" |
+| `list_trigger_bindings` | `GET /trigger-bindings` | "what triggers the gold suite?" |
+| `get_notification_config` | `GET /suites/{id}/notifications` | "who gets alerted for this suite?" |
+| `get_suite_performance` | `GET /dashboard/summary` (per-suite slice) | "which suite is worst this month?" |
+| `export_suite` | `GET /suites/{id}/export` | "export the orders suite" |
+
+**Tier 2 — mutating, edit-permission-gated:**
+
+| Candidate tool | Wraps | Note |
+|---|---|---|
+| `dryrun_check` | `POST /suites/{id}/checks/dryrun` | the LLM author-preview loop — pairs with `create_check` |
+| `update_check` / `delete_check` | `PATCH`/`DELETE` check | delete needs confirm-style ergonomics |
+| `snooze_check` / `unsnooze_check` | check snooze endpoints | "snooze this alert for 24h" |
+| `cancel_run` | `POST /runs/{id}/cancel` | |
+| `create_schedule` / `delete_schedule` | schedules CRUD | "run this suite daily at 9am IST" |
+| `create_trigger_binding` | `POST /trigger-bindings` | "run this suite when the orders DAG succeeds" |
+| `import_suite` | `POST /suites/import` | |
+| `suggest_column_policy` | `POST /suites/{id}/column-policy/suggest` | redaction-policy assistant |
+| `test_connection` | `POST /connections/{id}/test` | action but non-destructive |
+
+**Excluded (deliberate):** connection create/update/reauth (**credentials transiting an
+LLM — hard no**); the orchestration webhooks (M2M surface); admin endpoints (workspace-
+admin scoping pending #488); share mutations (permission escalation through a
+conversational interface); `_probe` (demo-only); `/me` + `/users/search` (low value).
+
+---
+
 ## How this maps to GitHub
 
 - **Status** lives on the GitHub `Backlog (post-v1 / testing)` milestone — this doc mirrors it by theme.

+|---|---|---|
+| `list_checks` / `get_check` | `GET /suites/{id}/checks[/{id}]` | "what checks does the orders suite have?" |
+| `get_check_history` | `GET .../checks/{id}/history` | "how has null-rate trended this week?" |
+| `list_runs` | `GET /runs` (filterable) | "show me yesterday's failed runs" |
+| `get_run_results` | `GET /runs/{id}` (redacted) | "why did run X fail?" |
+| `list_connections` | `GET /connections` — names/types/health **only, never config or secrets** | "which connections are unhealthy?" |
+| `list_schedules` | `GET /schedules` | "when does the orders suite run?" |
+| `list_trigger_bindings` | `GET /trigger-bindings` | "what triggers the gold suite?" |
+| `get_notification_config` | `GET /suites/{id}/notifications` | "who gets alerted for this suite?" |
+| `get_suite_performance` | `GET /dashboard/summary` (per-suite slice) | "which suite is worst this month?" |
+| `export_suite` | `GET /suites/{id}/export` | "export the orders suite" |
+
+**Tier 2 — mutating, edit-permission-gated:**
+
+| Candidate tool | Wraps | Note |
+|---|---|---|
+| `dryrun_check` | `POST /suites/{id}/checks/dryrun` | the LLM author-preview loop — pairs with `create_check` |
+| `update_check` / `delete_check` | `PATCH`/`DELETE` check | delete needs confirm-style ergonomics |
+| `snooze_check` / `unsnooze_check` | check snooze endpoints | "snooze this alert for 24h" |
+| `cancel_run` | `POST /runs/{id}/cancel` | |
+| `create_schedule` / `delete_schedule` | schedules CRUD | "run this suite daily at 9am IST" |
+| `create_trigger_binding` | `POST /trigger-bindings` | "run this suite when the orders DAG succeeds" |
+| `import_suite` | `POST /suites/import` | |
+| `suggest_column_policy` | `POST /suites/{id}/column-policy/suggest` | redaction-policy assistant |
+| `test_connection` | `POST /connections/{id}/test` | action but non-destructive |
+
+**Excluded (deliberate):** connection create/update/reauth (**credentials transiting an
+LLM — hard no**); the orchestration webhooks (M2M surface); admin endpoints (workspace-
+admin scoping pending #488); share mutations (permission escalation through a
+conversational interface); `_probe` (demo-only); `/me` + `/users/search` (low value).
+
+---
+
 ## How this maps to GitHub
 
 - **Status** lives on the GitHub `Backlog (post-v1 / testing)` milestone — this doc mirrors it by theme.

file.content:
def _suite_performance(
    session: Session, accessible: Select[tuple[uuid.UUID]]
) -> list[SuitePerformance]:
    """Per-suite health from each suite's **latest** run, worst (lowest) first.

    A suite with no run, or whose latest run wrote no results (a hard-failed run
    rolls back its results), is omitted — there is no health to show.
    """
    # DISTINCT ON (suite_id) ordered by created_at desc → the latest run per suite.
    latest = (
        select(Run.id, Run.suite_id)
        .where(Run.suite_id.in_(accessible))
        .order_by(Run.suite_id, Run.created_at.desc())
        .distinct(Run.suite_id)
        .subquery()
    )
    stmt = (
        select(Suite.id, Suite.name, Result.status, func.count())
        .select_from(latest)
        .join(Suite, Suite.id == latest.c.suite_id)
        .join(Result, Result.run_id == latest.c.id)
        .group_by(Suite.id, Suite.name, Result.status)
    )
    counts: dict[uuid.UUID, dict[str, int]] = {}
    names: dict[uuid.UUID, str] = {}
    for sid, name, status, count in session.execute(stmt):
        counts.setdefault(sid, {})[status] = count
        names[sid] = name

    out = [
        SuitePerformance(
            suite_id=sid,
            name=names[sid],
            score=health_score(c),
            state=performance_state(health_score(c)),
        )
        for sid, c in counts.items()
    ]
    # Worst first (lowest score), suites with no severity result (score None) last.
    out.sort(key=lambda s: (s.score is None, s.score if s.score is not None else 0.0))
    return out


def _active_connections(session: Session, accessible: Select[tuple[uuid.UUID]]) -> int:
    """Distinct connections referenced by the caller's accessible suites."""

content.content:
Idempotent on the `triggered_by` seed markers, so re-running adds nothing.
    Gives the Results page real content spanning the full result vocabulary —
    pass | warn | fail | critical | error | skip across the two succeeded runs —

    mixed_marker = "seed:run:mixed"
    failed_marker = "seed:run:failed"
    existing = set(
        session.scalars(
            select(Run.triggered_by).where(
                Run.suite_id == suite.id,
                Run.triggered_by.in_([succeeded_marker, mixed_marker, failed_marker]),
            )
        )
    )
    now = datetime.now(UTC)
    created = 0
    checks = {c.name: c for c in session.scalars(select(Check).where(Check.suite_id == suite.id))}

    if succeeded_marker not in existing:
        _seed_result_run(
            session,
            suite=suite,
            checks=checks,
            marker=succeeded_marker,
            results=_SEED_RUN_RESULTS,

            finished_at=now - timedelta(minutes=4, seconds=48),
        )
        created += 1
    else:
        # Backfill (idempotent): DBs seeded before `sample_failures` rode the
        # seed keep their existing run (the marker guard skips it), so attach
        # the sample to the already-seeded fail result — the run-detail sample
        # drill-down then works on old stacks without wiping runs first.

        if fail_check is not None and fail_sample is not None:
            run_id = session.scalars(
                select(Run.id).where(Run.suite_id == suite.id, Run.triggered_by == succeeded_marker)
            ).first()
            stale = session.scalars(
                select(Result).where(
                    Result.run_id == run_id,
                    Result.check_id == fail_check.id,
                    Result.sample_failures.is_(None),

            ).first()
            if stale is not None:
                stale.sample_failures = fail_sample

    if mixed_marker not in existing:
        _seed_result_run(

            suite=suite,
            checks=checks,
            marker=mixed_marker,
            results=_SEED_RUN_MIXED_RESULTS,

        )
        created += 1

input.new_string:
if failed_marker not in existing:
        session.add(
            Run(
                suite_id=suite.id,
                status="failed",
                triggered_by=failed_marker,
                created_at=now - timedelta(minutes=6),
                started_at=now - timedelta(minutes=6),
                finished_at=now - timedelta(minutes=5, seconds=58),
            )
        )
        created += 1

    # Normalize seed-run timestamps on EVERY reseed (existing rows included):
    # keeps the runs inside the dashboard's 7-day window on old stacks, and
    # keeps the operational-failure run (no results) OLDER than the
    # result-bearing runs — `suite_performance` reads each suite's LATEST run,
    # so a newest run without results would blank the Suite Performance panel.
    offsets: dict[str, tuple[timedelta, timedelta]] = {
        failed_marker: (timedelta(minutes=6), timedelta(minutes=5, seconds=58)),
        succeeded_marker: (timedelta(minutes=5), timedelta(minutes=4, seconds=48)),
        mixed_marker: (timedelta(minutes=3, seconds=30), timedelta(minutes=3, seconds=18)),
    }
    seed_runs = session.scalars(
        select(Run).where(Run.suite_id == suite.id, Run.triggered_by.in_(list(offsets)))
    )
    for run in seed_runs:
        start_offset, finish_offset = offsets[str(run.triggered_by)]
        run.created_at = now - start_offset
        run.started_at = now - start_offset
        run.finished_at = now - finish_offset

    return created

content.text:
Review target: `527`

`low effort → 1 diff pass → no verify → ≤4 findings`

## Turn 1 — read

One tool call: read the unified diff (`git diff @{upstream}...HEAD; git diff HEAD`
to cover both committed and uncommitted changes, or `git diff main...HEAD` /
the target passed as an argument). Skip test/fixture
hunks (`test/`, `spec/`, `__tests__/`, `*_test.*`, `*.test.*`,
`fixtures/`, `testdata/`) — test-file changes are not reviewed at this level.
No subagents, no full-file reads.

## Turn 2 — findings

Flag runtime-correctness bugs visible from the hunk alone: inverted/wrong
condition, off-by-one, null/undefined deref where adjacent lines show the value
can be absent, removed guard, falsy-zero check, missing `await`,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in a catch that should propagate.
Also flag — still from the hunk alone — new code that duplicates an existing
helper visible in the diff context, and dead code the diff leaves behind.

Do **not** flag style, naming, perf, missing tests, or anything outside the
hunk.

Output at most **4 findings**, most-severe first, one line each:
`path/to/file.ext:123 — what's wrong and the concrete failure`. If nothing
qualifies, output exactly `(none)`.

input.command: gh pr diff 527 | grep -v "^diff --git a/frontend/e2e" | sed -n '1,150p'
input.description: Read PR 527 diff (non-spec hunks)

content.content:
+        # seed keep their existing run (the marker guard skips it), so attach
+        # the sample to the already-seeded fail result — the run-detail sample
+        # drill-down then works on old stacks without wiping runs first.
+        fail_check = checks.get("status in set")
+        fail_sample = next((r[5] for r in _SEED_RUN_RESULTS if r[0] == "status in set"), None)
+        if fail_check is not None and fail_sample is not None:
+            run_id = session.scalars(
+                select(Run.id).where(Run.suite_id == suite.id, Run.triggered_by == succeeded_marker)
+            ).first()
+            stale = session.scalars(
+                select(Result).where(
+                    Result.run_id == run_id,
+                    Result.check_id == fail_check.id,
+                    Result.sample_failures.is_(None),
+                )
+            ).first()
+            if stale is not None:
+                stale.sample_failures = fail_sample
 
     if mixed_marker not in existing:
         _seed_result_run(
@@ -440,13 +507,32 @@ def _seed_runs(session: Session, *, suite: Suite) -> int:
                 suite_id=suite.id,
                 status="failed",
                 triggered_by=failed_marker,
-                created_at=now - timedelta(minutes=2),
-                started_at=now - timedelta(minutes=2),
-                finished_at=now - timedelta(minutes=1, seconds=58),
+                created_at=now - timedelta(minutes=6),
+                started_at=now - timedelta(minutes=6),
+                finished_at=now - timedelta(minutes=5, seconds=58),
             )
         )
         created += 1
 
+    # Normalize seed-run timestamps on EVERY reseed (existing rows included):
+    # keeps the runs inside the dashboard's 7-day window on old stacks, and
+    # keeps the operational-failure run (no results) OLDER than the
+    # result-bearing runs — `suite_performance` reads each suite's LATEST run,
+    # so a newest run without results would blank the Suite Performance panel.
+    offsets: dict[str, tuple[timedelta, timedelta]] = {
+        failed_marker: (timedelta(minutes=6), timedelta(minutes=5, seconds=58)),
+        succeeded_marker: (timedelta(minutes=5), timedelta(minutes=4, seconds=48)),
+        mixed_marker: (timedelta(minutes=3, seconds=30), timedelta(minutes=3, seconds=18)),
+    }
+    seed_runs = session.scalars(
+        select(Run).where(Run.suite_id == suite.id, Run.triggered_by.in_(list(offsets)))
+    )
+    for run in seed_runs:
+        start_offset, finish_offset = offsets[str(run.triggered_by)]
+        run.created_at = now - start_offset
+        run.started_at = now - start_offset
+        run.finished_at = now - finish_offset
+
     return created

+import { expect, test } from '@playwright/test';
+
+// Workspace-admin surfaces (#289 + ADR 0027): the dev-bypass identity is in
+// WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS (compose + CI), so /admin and /settings are reachable
+// and the admin-only footer nav renders. The webhook-config surface (#493) —
+// the supported way to obtain the ADF/Airflow inbound webhook URLs instead of
+// hand-assembling them (#92) — lives on Settings → Webhooks.
+test.describe('Admin control centre', () => {
+  test('renders the unscoped suites / members / access tables', async ({ page }) => {
+    await page.goto('/admin');
+    await expect(page.getByRole('heading', { name: 'Admin', level: 3 })).toBeVisible();
+
+    // Scope to the content area — the sidebar nav also carries a 'Suites'
+    // link, which would satisfy an unscoped getByText even with Admin broken.
+    const main = page.getByRole('main');
+    for (const section of ['Suites', 'Members', 'Access grants']) {
+      await expect(main.getByText(section, { exact: true }).first()).toBeVisible();
+    }
+    // Unscoped visibility: the seeded suite and the resolved admin identity.

content.content: Launching skill: code-review

content.text:
Review target: `531`

`low effort → 1 diff pass → no verify → ≤4 findings`

## Turn 1 — read

One tool call: read the unified diff (`git diff @{upstream}...HEAD; git diff HEAD`
to cover both committed and uncommitted changes, or `git diff main...HEAD` /
the target passed as an argument). Skip test/fixture
hunks (`test/`, `spec/`, `__tests__/`, `*_test.*`, `*.test.*`,
`fixtures/`, `testdata/`) — test-file changes are not reviewed at this level.
No subagents, no full-file reads.

## Turn 2 — findings

Flag runtime-correctness bugs visible from the hunk alone: inverted/wrong
condition, off-by-one, null/undefined deref where adjacent lines show the value
can be absent, removed guard, falsy-zero check, missing `await`,
wrong-variable copy-paste, error swallowed in a catch that should propagate.
Also flag — still from the hunk alone — new code that duplicates an existing
helper visible in the diff context, and dead code the diff leaves behind.

Do **not** flag style, naming, perf, missing tests, or anything outside the
hunk.

Output at most **4 findings**, most-severe first, one line each:
`path/to/file.ext:123 — what's wrong and the concrete failure`. If nothing
qualifies, output exactly `(none)`.
